name: Les Spann
abstract: Leslie Spann Jr. (May 23, 1932 – January 24, 1989) was an American jazz guitarist and flautist.

concerts.label: WLIB Festival of Music and Drama

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Before that there was no saxophone player, it was Les Spann playing guitar and flute... And he and Dizzy did a lot of flute and muted trumpet duets... One album we did called “Have Trumpet Will Excite” features that a lot, the two of them
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Did you ever hear of Les Spann? ... I need to talk to Airto about that guy. They probably know. They were like a clique together, he and Jobim and that Flora Purim and Milton Nascimento, those guys. They hung out together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: He happened to be in the Army with Les Spann... So we got to be friends across that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Les Spann, you know. But it was such a good band. You know, we memorized the show?

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Les Spann. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Les Spann. All of them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
